metadata.title: County Property Tax Rates and Veteran Exemptions — IA
metadata.description: County-level property tax rates, veteran exemption implementation details, and application information for Iowa metro area counties.
sources: Iowa Department of Revenue — Property Tax Division
U.S. Census Bureau, American Community Survey

notes.tax_rate_format: Effective rate as decimal fraction of market value (e.g. 0.0151 = 1.51%).
notes.scope: Des Moines, Cedar Rapids, Iowa City metro — Polk, Linn, Johnson counties.
notes.update_cycle: Assessed values set biennially (odd years). Levies set annually by counties, cities, school districts.
notes.assessment_method: Iowa assesses residential property at market value, then applies a statewide rollback (47.43% for 2026) to determine taxable value. Levy rates are per $1,000 of taxable value. The _computed effective_rate is expressed on market value (median taxes paid / median home value), netting out the rollback.
notes.veteran_credit: Disabled Veteran's Homestead Tax Credit (Iowa Code Ch. 425) — 100% property tax credit for 100% P&T or IU disabled veterans. Military Service Tax Exemption (Iowa Code §426A) — $1,852 taxable value reduction for eligible veterans.

metadata._comment: Iowa county property tax data. Iowa uses a rollback system that reduces taxable value to ~47% of market value, but _computed fields are expressed on market value for consumer consistency.

veteran_exemption_state_law.statute: Iowa Code Ch. 425 (Disabled Veteran's Homestead Tax Credit), §426A (Military Service Tax Exemption)
veteran_exemption_state_law.description: 100% property tax credit for veterans with 100% permanent and total service-connected disability or individual unemployability. Effectively a full exemption — the entire tax bill is credited. Separate Military Service Tax Exemption reduces taxable value by $1,852 for any eligible veteran.
veteran_exemption_state_law.surviving_spouse_conditions: Unremarried surviving spouse retains the credit

property_tax.assessment_method: Market value with statewide residential rollback (47.43% for 2026)
property_tax.homestead_credit_note: Iowa Homestead Tax Credit reduces tax by up to $4,850 of taxable value for owner-occupied primary residences

disabled_veteran_homestead_tax_credit.eligibility: 100% permanent and total service-connected disability or IU determination
disabled_veteran_homestead_tax_credit.benefit: 100% property tax credit (full tax bill credited)
disabled_veteran_homestead_tax_credit.application: File with county assessor; attach VA award letter and DD214

military_service_tax_exemption.eligibility: Any veteran who served on active duty during a recognized conflict or 20+ years reserve/guard service
military_service_tax_exemption.application: File with county assessor; one-time application

application.deadline: File with county assessor by December 31 of the tax year
